metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1052
metadata.comment:
Rationale: SynArfGEF/BRAG3/IQSEC3 is a synaptic guanine nucleotide exchange factor present in inhibitory postsynapses with the capability to activate Arf6. IQSEC3 interacts with gephrin at inhibitory synapses (Fig. 1, 2, 4) and overexpression and knock-down experiments in cultured neurons indicate that IQSEC3 is involved in the regulation of synapse development and this activity is dependent on the Arf-GEF activity of IQSEC3 (Fig. 5, 8,).

The referenced paper demonstrates that IQSEC3 activates Arf6 and localizes to inhibitory synapses.

5/7/2017 Pim
- SynArfGEF/BRAG3/IQSEC3 over-expression is shown to affect the number of DIV10 neuron synapses (Fig.5). Interaction of SynArfGEF/BRAG3/IQSEC3 with the postsynaptic marker protein Gephyrin is needed for this effect (Fig.7). This effect does rely on the ArfGEF activity of SynArfGEF/BRAG3/IQSEC3 (Fig.8). Fig.10 shows effect shRNA SynArfGEF/BRAG3/IQSEC3 KD on postsynaptic specialization size. Together this can be used to justify the term:

"positive regulation of ARF protein signal transduction (GO:0032014)"
occurs_in "postsynapse (GO:0098794)"
part_of "postsynapse organization (GO:0050808)"

29/5/2018 Pim
- Because SynArfGEF/BRAG3/IQSEC3 interacts with gephyrin and is essential in the postsynaptic recruitment of this protein I have annotated to the more specific term 'postsynapse organization (GO:0099173)' instead of 'synapse organization (GO:0050808)'.. Experimental description: Expression plasmids for fragments of rat IQSEC3 (GenBankTM accession number, NM_207617) were prepared by amplifying...
